questions.prompt: How do you say 'Hello' (daytime) in French?
questions.explanation: 'Bonjour' is the all-purpose daytime greeting. 'Bonne nuit' is good night, 'Au revoir' is goodbye, 'Merci' is thank you.

questions.prompt: Which word means 'thank you'?
questions.explanation: 'Merci' = thank you. 'S'il vous plaît' = please, 'Pardon' = sorry/excuse me, 'De rien' = you're welcome.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Je ___ Marie.' (My name is Marie — using s'appeler)
questions.explanation: 'Je m'appelle Marie' literally = 'I call myself Marie'. It's the standard way to give your name.

questions.prompt: Which article is correct: '___ table' (table is feminine)?
questions.explanation: 'Table' is feminine, so it takes 'la'. Always learn nouns WITH their article: 'la table'.

questions.prompt: Write the number 'three' in French.
questions.explanation: 0-5: zéro, un, deux, trois, quatre, cinq. 'Trois' = three.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form of être: 'Je ___ étudiant.'
questions.explanation: être: je suis, tu es, il est, nous sommes, vous êtes, ils sont. With 'je' it's 'suis'.

questions.prompt: Fill in avoir: 'Elle ___ deux frères.' (She has two brothers.)
questions.explanation: avoir: j'ai, tu as, il/elle a... With 'elle' the form is 'a'. Don't confuse it with 'à' (the preposition).

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to say 'I would like a coffee, please.'
questions.answer: Je voudrais un café s'il vous plaît
questions.explanation: 'Je voudrais' (I would like) is the polite way to order. Word order mirrors English here.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'How are you?' (formal)?
questions.explanation: 'Comment allez-vous ?' uses 'aller' (to go) idiomatically for wellbeing. The others ask your name, where you're going, and your age.

questions.prompt: Say your age structure: to say 'I am 20 years old', French uses the verb avoir. Write the full sentence for 20.
questions.explanation: French expresses age with AVOIR, not être: 'J'ai vingt ans' = literally 'I have 20 years'. This trips up every English speaker.

questions.prompt: Conjugate the -ER verb: 'Je ___ français.' (parler)
questions.explanation: Regular -ER endings: je parle, tu parles, il parle, nous parlons, vous parlez, ils parlent. With 'je' → 'parle'.

questions.prompt: Make it negative: 'Je ___ comprends ___.' (I don't understand)
questions.explanation: Negation wraps the verb: ne + verb + pas. 'Je ne comprends pas.'

questions.prompt: Complete with aller: 'Nous ___ à Paris.' (We are going to Paris.)
questions.explanation: aller: je vais, tu vas, il va, nous allons, vous allez, ils vont. With 'nous' → 'allons'.

questions.prompt: Which is the 'near future' of 'I am going to eat'?
questions.explanation: Futur proche = aller (conjugated) + infinitive. 'Je vais manger.' 'J'ai mangé' is past; 'Je mangerai' is the simple future.

questions.prompt: Form a question with est-ce que: rearrange 'tu parles anglais' into a question.
questions.explanation: Add 'Est-ce que' to the front of a statement to make a yes/no question: 'Est-ce que tu parles anglais ?'

questions.prompt: Choose the correct passé composé: 'Hier, j'___ visité le musée.'
questions.explanation: Most verbs form the past with avoir + participle: 'j'ai visité'. 'visiter' takes avoir, not être.

questions.prompt: Which verb takes ÊTRE in the passé composé?
questions.explanation: A small set of movement/change verbs use être (aller, venir, partir...): 'Je suis allé(e)'. Most others use avoir.

questions.prompt: Agreement: complete 'Elle est ___.' (She went — aller, with agreement)
questions.explanation: With être, the participle agrees with the subject. Feminine singular adds -e: 'Elle est allée'.

questions.prompt: Order the words: 'I don't want to go to the restaurant.'
questions.answer: Je ne veux pas aller au restaurant
questions.explanation: ne…pas wraps the conjugated verb (veux), and the infinitive (aller) follows. 'à + le' contracts to 'au'.

questions.prompt: Pick the correct partitive: 'Je voudrais ___ eau.' (some water; eau is feminine, starts with a vowel)
questions.explanation: Partitive 'some': du (m), de la (f), de l' (before a vowel), des (plural). 'Eau' starts with a vowel, so 'de l'eau'.

questions.prompt: Which tense describes an ongoing/background past state: 'Quand j'étais petit, je ___ au foot.' (to play habitually)
questions.explanation: The imparfait (jouais) describes habits and background in the past ('used to play'). 'ai joué' (passé composé) is for single completed events.

questions.prompt: Imparfait of être for 'il': 'Il ___ tard.' (It was late.)
questions.explanation: être is irregular in the imparfait but uses the normal endings on the stem 'ét-': j'étais, tu étais, il était...

questions.prompt: Choose the right past tense: 'Je regardais la télé quand le téléphone ___.' (rang — a single event)
questions.explanation: Background action (regardais = imparfait) is interrupted by a single completed event (a sonné = passé composé). This contrast is the core A2 skill.

questions.prompt: Replace the object with a pronoun: 'Je vois Marie.' → 'Je ___ vois.'
questions.explanation: Marie is a feminine direct object, replaced by 'la' before the verb: 'Je la vois.' 'lui' is an indirect object (to her).

questions.prompt: Use the right relative pronoun (subject): 'C'est le livre ___ est sur la table.'
questions.explanation: 'qui' replaces the SUBJECT of the relative clause (the book IS on the table). 'que' replaces the object.

questions.prompt: Match each infinitive to its past participle.
questions.explanation: Irregular participles must be memorised: faire→fait, prendre→pris, voir→vu, être→été. They appear constantly in the passé composé.

questions.prompt: Comparative: 'Ce livre est ___ intéressant que l'autre.' (more interesting than)
questions.explanation: Comparative of superiority = plus + adjective + que. 'plus intéressant que' = more interesting than.

questions.prompt: Express duration: 'J'apprends le français ___ deux ans.' (for two years, still ongoing)
questions.explanation: 'depuis' + present expresses an action that started in the past and continues now: 'depuis deux ans' = for (the last) two years.

questions.prompt: Order the words: 'I got up early and I had breakfast.' (reflexive + passé composé)
questions.answer: Je me suis levé tôt et j'ai pris le petit-déjeuner
questions.explanation: Reflexive verbs take être in the passé composé ('je me suis levé'), while 'prendre' takes avoir ('j'ai pris').

questions.prompt: Futur simple of 'aller' for 'nous': 'Demain, nous ___ à la plage.'
questions.explanation: aller has an irregular future stem 'ir-': j'irai, tu iras, il ira, nous irons... 'allons' is present, 'irions' is conditional.
